title: bonjeanea hirsuta var. incana
intro: Bonjeanea hirsuta var. incana, also known as the hairy Bonjeanea, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. It is a type of climber that is native to tropical Africa, where it is commonly found in the wild. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its use in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The hairy Bonjeana can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas and infusions, which are believed to provide relief from pain and inflammation, as well as boost the immune system.
